title: Incursa.Quic Stream Capacity Callback Work Item
summary: Promote the stream-capacity callback slice on top of the real connection/runtime seam while keeping the public stream surface honest.
planned_changes:
- Add the public `QuicStreamCapacityChangedArgs` type.
- Add `QuicConnectionOptions.StreamCapacityCallback`.
- Wire the initial peer stream-capacity delta from `QuicConnectionRuntime` into `QuicConnection` and listener-accepted connections.
- Add truthful later peer `MAX_STREAMS` growth reporting on the supported active loopback path without turning the callback into a general event pipeline.
- Keep the callback silent when the supported boundary is not reached or the connection is canceled or disposed before peer limits commit.
- Keep `QuicStream` write/completion behavior unchanged unless a truthful minimum expansion becomes necessary.
- Reconcile the public API analyzer file, trace artifacts, and requirement-home tests with the approved subset.
verification_plan: Use API-diff, reflection, and focused requirement-home tests to prove the callback surface exists, the initial peer delta is observed once on the supported loopback path, later real MAX_STREAMS growth is observed on the supported active loopback path, zero-delta and pre-commit cases remain silent, cancellation/disposal stay honest, and the public boundary remains narrow. No write-side expansion is expected for this slice.
completion_notes: This item keeps the stream-capacity callback narrow and truthful for both the initial delta and later real MAX_STREAMS growth on the supported loopback path. Stream-close-driven capacity release, reset/stop-sending parity, and broader write-heavy stream behavior remain separate slices.
